title: Vendor latest mono repo for libartifact changes
body:
This PR vendors in the strong typed libartifact code and carries the associated changes.

<!--

For more detailed information, please review our contributing guidelines:

#### Checklist

Ensure you have completed the following checklist for your pull request to be reviewed:
<!-- Use [x] to mark as done, or click the checkbox after opening PR -->

- [x] Certify you wrote the patch or otherwise have the right to pass it on as an open-source patch by signing all
commits. (`git commit -s`). (If needed, use `git commit -s --amend`).  The author email must match
the sign-off email address. See [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#sign-your-prs)
for more information.
- [ ] Referenced issues using `Fixes: #00000` in commit message (if applicable)
- [x] [Tests](https://github.com/containers/podman/tree/main/test#readme) have been added/updated (or no tests are needed)
- [x] [Documentation](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/README.md) has been updated (or no documentation changes are needed)
- [x] All commits pass `make validatepr` (format/lint checks)
- [x] [Release note](https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/guide/release-notes.md) entered in the section below (or `None` if no user-facing changes)

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

Include "action required" if users need to take action when upgrading.
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *baude* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [baude] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
[NON-BLOCKING] Packit jobs failed. @containers/packit-build please check. Everyone else, feel free to ignore.

a bunch of test failures now since we only accept a full name.


Also
```
<+021ms> # $ podman  artifact add a-t423-uibk1jts /etc/hosts
<+094ms> # Error: repository name must be canonical
```

Should that error be more verbose, I am not sure a user know what the canonical format is supposed to be? Should it say fully qualified name?
cc @mtrmac

> Should that error be more verbose, I am not sure a user know what the canonical format is supposed to be? Should it say fully qualified name?

Fwiw, if the preference is something like that, the error is defined so it can be caught by type and either wrapped or have a different response.  I'd be happy to do this in libartifact if there is agreement that this is preferred.

(LGTM, for the record)

> ```
> <+021ms> # $ podman  artifact add a-t423-uibk1jts /etc/hosts
> <+094ms> # Error: repository name must be canonical
> ```
> 
> Should that error be more verbose, I am not sure a user know what the canonical format is supposed to be? Should it say fully qualified name?

Good idea. (The reported error is “I parsed your input successfully as `docker.io/library/add a-t423-uibk1jts` and this API requires you to use exactly that syntax, not shorthands”, hence “canonical”.)

For users, “canonical” is definitely not helpful (jargon, and even ambiguous as to whether “canonical” is `docker.io/library/busybox` or `busybox`). “Fully-qualified” would be accurate but might not be obvious either, it’s sort of our internal Podman shorthand.

Maybe saying something vaguely like “must start a hostname component” (or with "${hostname}/"… it’s difficult to express literal / variable in plain text).
